free_text: Analytical use of the multiple gamma-rays from
C-12(D,P)C-13* nuclear reaction.
Last checking has been done.
Thin carbon target target had a thickness equivalent to
a proton energy loss of 2-KeV at 3-MeV up to 4-KeV at
1-MeV.
Numerical relationship between the gamma-ray
yield and the element distribution in a thick target.
A 50-mm diameter, 59-mm long HPGE DETECTOR,
with an efficiency of 22.7% relative to NAI(TL) AND
with an energy resolution of 1.7-KeV FWHM AT 1.33-MEV.
EN-RSL-FW The Overall energy resolution.
A 2-MV pelletron 6SDH2
.Data-point reader uncertainty.
.Data-point reader uncertainty.
The accuracy of the absolute value of these cross
sections depends on the homogeneity of the target-2%,
on the value of the target thickness-5%, the detector
absolute efficiency-5-10%. All in all, the absolute
error is around +-10%, mainly due to the nature of the
target.
For the 3089-KeV peak, the relative error is +-1% to
+-2%, while for the 3684 and 3854-KeV peaks, it is
+-5% at 2-MeV and +-2% at 3-MeV.
.By CAJAD.

- By compiler. The authors measured gamma-rays yield at
135 degree to the beam.  But they gave cross sections
as millibarn.  It is unknown used method to calculate
total cross section from one angle measurements.
